items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: It's also one of the sliding-doors types of situations for the Ja/JJJ/Bane-era Grizzlies, who (if memory serves) were reportedly heavily in on both OG and Bridges to the tune of multiple firsts, but weren't able to get those deals over the finish line. Hard players to get, expensive to keep.

items.title: [Vorkunov] The Jazz are the only team that earned top-5 picks in each of the last two lotteries, but the Memphis Grizzlies own Utah’s 2027 first-round pick as part of the Jaren Jackson Jr. trade ... Under the new rules, the Grizzlies’ return in the trade would be capped at the No. 6 pick next season
items.author: cleo22270
items.body_excerpt: Article: NBA’s lottery proposal changes draft pick values. Many have already been traded The Jazz are the only team that earned top-5 picks in each of the last two lotteries, but the Memphis Grizzlies own Utah’s 2027 first-round pick as part of the Jaren Jackson Jr. trade this…

items.title: Would Grizzlies have done JJJ trade if they knew they couldn't get top-5 pick with it? I don't know. Grizzlies seem to be only team impacted by the restrictions.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Would Grizzlies have done JJJ trade if they knew they couldn't get top-5 pick with it? I don't know. Grizzlies seem to be only team impacted by the restrictions.

Rules also won’t prevent a team from picking No. 1 pick in two straight seasons with different team picks www.nytimes.com/athletic/730...
